name: Family First
description_short: Family First Federal Credit Union offers traditional banking services including checking, savings, loans, and credit cards to its members with rates starting as low as 3.99% APR on auto loans.
description_long:
Family First Federal Credit Union is an established financial cooperative that has served its membership for at least 70 years, as evidenced by their recent 70th Annual Membership Meeting. As a credit union, the organization operates on a member-owned cooperative model rather than as a for-profit institution, which typically translates to competitive rates and member-focused policies.

The credit union provides a comprehensive suite of consumer banking services including multiple checking and savings account options (Preferred Checking, Select Checking, Plus Checking, Basics Checking, and Share Accounts), certificates of deposit, and a full range of loan products. Their lending offerings span auto loans (new and used vehicles, recreational vehicles) with rates as low as 3.99% APR, mortgages with flexible terms, home equity loans, personal loans starting at 8.49% APR, credit cards at 12.90% APR, and secured loan options. They also offer ancillary services like free e-bill pay, free debit cards, ATM access, phone banking, wire transfers, money orders, and US savings bonds.

Family First distinguishes itself through its credit union structure, which typically means members have voting rights and access to member-exclusive rates and products. The organization emphasizes accessibility with multiple account tiers to serve different financial profiles and provides free digital banking tools including online banking, mobile app access, and phone banking services. Their competitive auto loan rates and flexible mortgage messaging suggest an emphasis on major life purchases.

The primary limitation is the lack of detailed information about membership eligibility requirements, which is standard for credit unions but not transparently presented on their homepage. Additionally, while rates are mentioned for select products, the full rate schedules and specific terms are not visible without accessing their rates page or contacting the institution directly. The website provides limited transparency regarding minimum deposits, account fees beyond a referenced fee schedule, or qualification requirements for their lowest advertised rates.
services: Auto loans (new and used vehicles, recreational vehicles)
Free debit cards and ATM cards
pros: Auto loan rates as low as 3.99% APR for new and used vehicles
Multiple checking account options (6 varieties) to match different member needs and balances
Free digital banking services including e-bill pay, debit cards, phone banking, and mobile app
Comprehensive loan portfolio including mortgages, home equity, personal, and secured loans
Credit union structure typically offers member voting rights and profit-sharing benefits
Established institution with 70+ year operational history
Personal loan rates starting at 8.49% APR
cons: Membership eligibility criteria not clearly stated on homepage, creating potential barriers to access
Credit card APR of 12.90% is relatively high compared to many national card issuers
Limited rate transparency—only sample rates shown; full schedule requires navigation to separate page
Website lacks details on minimum deposits, account fees, or credit requirements for advertised rates
No information visible about business loan options despite 'Business Accounts' menu item
best_for: Credit union members seeking competitive auto financing and mortgages
Consumers wanting multiple checking account tiers based on their balance and activity level
Individuals valuing free digital banking tools and member-owned financial institution benefits
Borrowers looking for flexible home loan and home equity options
diagnosis: Family First Federal Credit Union is best for existing or eligible members seeking competitive rates on auto loans and mortgages within a member-owned cooperative structure. The main caveat is that membership eligibility is not clearly defined on their website, and prospective members should verify they qualify before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: The Family First — Great Falls, MT Credit Union | CreditDoc
meta_description: The Family First — NCUA-insured credit union in Great Falls, MT. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
